components.recommended_action: Migrate to Jira Cloud outgoing webhook with equivalent payload
components.report_text: Standard outgoing webhook with auth header and retry — fully migratable.
components.original_script:
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import java.net.http.HttpClient
import java.net.http.HttpRequest
import java.net.http.HttpResponse

def payload = new JsonBuilder([
    issueKey: issue.key,
    status: issue.status.name,
    assignee: issue.assignee?.name,
    priority: issue.priority.name,
    timestamp: new Date().toInstant().toString()
]).toString()

def client = HttpClient.newHttpClient()
def request = HttpRequest.newBuilder()
    .uri(URI.create('https://hooks.internal.example.com/jira-events'))
    .header('Content-Type', 'application/json')
    .header('Authorization', 'Bearer ${SECRET_TOKEN}')
    .POST(HttpRequest.BodyPublishers.ofString(payload))
    .build()

def response = client.send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString())
if (response.statusCode() >= 500) {
    Thread.sleep(2000)
    client.send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString())
}

components.recommended_action: Migrate to Jira Cloud Automation rule with equivalent trigger and actions
components.report_text: Native automation rule — fully compatible with Jira Cloud Automation.
